hero_summary: Shadd Araban is a branch of the Hijaz family, and its personality appears through a distinctive upper route and a clearer inner contrast, while the Hijaz color remains present in both foundation and sayr.
short_identity: A Hijaz branch with a clear upper route and a stronger auditory contrast than some other branches of the family.

quick_facts.karar: The tonic according to the chosen root
quick_facts.ghammaz: Its importance is determined by the route and the upper treatment
quick_facts.lower_jins: Jins Hijaz in the basic structure
quick_facts.upper_jins: An upper region with a more definite and contrasted route
quick_facts.character: Dramatic, differentiated, and more sharply contrasted in the jawab
quick_facts.common_start: It usually begins by stabilizing the Hijaz foundation, then reveals its personality through the upper route

what_is_it: Shadd Araban is read inside the Hijaz family as a branch that preserves the Hijaz essence, but gains its personality through an upper construction that is more definite and more contrasted than basic Hijaz.

lower.name: Jins Hijaz
lower.description: The lower Hijaz jins remains the basis that ties this branch to the Hijaz family.

upper.name: Contrasted upper region
upper.starts_on: Usually after the lower jins has been stabilized
upper.description: The personality of Shadd Araban appears through an upper route that is more clearly contrasted within the family.

jins_architecture.transition_note: The real difference between Shadd Araban and Hijaz appears when the upper region is built and tension is distributed there.

sayr.summary: Shadd Araban first stabilizes the Hijaz foundation, then moves into a more differentiated upper construction before returning to the tonic to confirm its family belonging.
resting_tones: Resting tones within the lower jins
Upper stations that show clearer contrast in this branch
motion_notes: The difference from Hijaz is defined through the upper structure
One should listen closely to how the jawab is opened and how it returns
common_path: Reveal the branch’s special upper contrast

transpose_explainer: When this branch is transposed to another root, the interval logic and the functions of the tones that give it its identity inside the Hijaz family must be preserved. The written form, accidentals, and notational range may change, but the difference between this branch and Hijaz must remain audible in the upper region and in the sayr.
historical_notes: Hijaz branches are commonly understood in modern teaching through their relation to the parent maqam and their difference from it in the upper region.
This branch is usually read inside the family more than as a separate system detached from Hijaz logic.
school_differences: The explanation of this branch may differ from one school or reference to another.
Some traditions emphasize the upper contrast, while others focus on the general structural reading.
common_confusions: Listing note names alone is not enough to understand this branch; one must pay attention to movement, repose, and upper coloring.
Similarity in the lower jins to Hijaz does not mean complete maqamic identity.
listening_notes: Start by comparing it with basic Hijaz.
Notice where the stronger auditory contrast appears in the upper region.
